description: Set up MDX infrastructure with Shiki syntax highlighting. Install @next/mdx, @mdx-js/loader, @mdx-js/react, @types/mdx, shiki, rehype-pretty-code (Shiki-based rehype plugin), and gray-matter (for frontmatter parsing). Configure next.config.ts with createMDX and rehype-pretty-code plugin. Create mdx-components.tsx at project root. Create lib/snippets.ts utility to read MDX files from content/snippets/ directory, parse frontmatter with gray-matter, and return typed snippet metadata. Update the Snippet interface to include tags, createdAt, updatedAt fields. Create content/snippets/ directory. Convert the existing 4 snippets from lib/data.ts into MDX files with frontmatter and multi-tab code blocks. Update app/snippets/[id]/page.tsx to dynamically import and render MDX content with Shiki-highlighted code blocks. Update app/snippets/page.tsx and components/SnippetSearch.tsx to use the new data source and support tag-based filtering. Keep the existing search by title/category and extend it to also match tags.
steps: Install dependencies: npm install @next/mdx @mdx-js/loader @mdx-js/react @types/mdx shiki rehype-pretty-code gray-matter

Update next.config.ts: convert to next.config.mjs (required for ESM createMDX import). Import createMDX from @next/mdx, configure pageExtensions to include mdx, add rehype-pretty-code as rehypePlugin with a Shiki theme (e.g. one-dark-pro for dark, github-light for light - use css-variables theme for dark mode compatibility). Wrap nextConfig with withMDX.

Create mdx-components.tsx at project root following the pattern from node_modules/next/dist/docs/01-app/02-guides/mdx.md. Define custom components: pre (to wrap with styling classes for the code container), code (to pass through Shiki-highlighted HTML).

Create lib/snippets.ts: define SnippetMeta interface (id, title, description, category, language, tags: string[], createdAt, updatedAt). Export getAllSnippets() that uses fs.readdirSync on content/snippets/, reads each .mdx file, parses frontmatter with gray-matter, returns SnippetMeta[]. Export getSnippetById(id) that returns metadata for a single snippet. Export getSnippetSlugs() for generateStaticParams.

Create content/snippets/ directory with 4 initial MDX files converted from lib/data.ts: splunk-error-aggregation.mdx, react-use-debounce.mdx, python-batch-summarizer.mdx, bash-log-rotator.mdx. Each MDX file has YAML frontmatter (title, description, category, language, tags, createdAt, updatedAt) and content with the code block using triple-backtick with language annotation (for Shiki highlighting). Add a brief explanation section and usage notes as markdown prose around the code.

Update app/snippets/[id]/page.tsx: import getAllSnippets/getSnippetById from lib/snippets. Use generateStaticParams with getSnippetSlugs(). Use dynamic import (await import(`@/content/snippets/${id}.mdx`)) to get the MDX component. Render the MDX component wrapped in a prose container with dark mode support. Keep CopyButton but extract code text from the MDX frontmatter or a dedicated code export in each MDX file.

Create a CodeBlock component (components/CodeBlock.tsx) that wraps pre/code with the existing styling (rounded container, language label header, CopyButton). The MDX components in mdx-components.tsx should map pre to this component. Include a multi-tab variant (components/CodeTabs.tsx) - a client component with tab state that shows different code panels (e.g., 'Code', 'Test', 'Usage'). Use this in MDX files via explicit import.

Update components/SnippetSearch.tsx: update the Snippet type import to SnippetMeta from lib/snippets. Add tags display (pill badges) on each snippet card. Extend the filter logic to also match against snippet.tags array. Add a tag filter bar above the grid showing all unique tags as clickable filter pills.
Update app/snippets/page.tsx: import getAllSnippets from lib/snippets instead of from lib/data. Pass the snippets to SnippetSearch.

Add a 'Copy as AI Prompt' button (components/CopyAsPrompt.tsx) - a client component similar to CopyButton that formats the snippet as: 'Use this pattern in my code: [code]. Follow these conventions: [description]. Language: [language].' Place it alongside the existing CopyButton on the snippet detail page.
Remove the old lib/data.ts file (no longer needed once all imports are updated).
Run npm run build to verify all pages generate statically with MDX content and Shiki highlighting.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build succeeds with all snippet pages listed as SSG routes
npm run lint passes with no errors
Each of the 4 initial MDX snippets renders with syntax-highlighted code blocks (Shiki classes in output HTML)
Snippet search page shows tags as pill badges on cards
Search filters match against title, category, AND tags
Tag filter pills appear above the snippet grid and are clickable
CopyButton still works on snippet detail pages
Copy as AI Prompt button formats the snippet correctly and copies to clipboard
Dark mode and light mode work correctly with syntax highlighting
CodeTabs component renders multi-tab code blocks when used in MDX
verification: npm run build - confirm all snippet routes are statically generated
npm run lint - confirm no lint errors
findings: 1. Turbopack (default in Next.js 16) does not support rehype plugins due to serialization requirements. Had to use --webpack flag in build command. 2. The css-variables theme is NOT bundled in shiki - must use bundled themes like github-dark or github-light. 3. The dynamic import pattern `await import('@/content/snippets/${id}.mdx')` works correctly with webpack for MDX files. 4. gray-matter parses YAML frontmatter from MDX files but the frontmatter is not rendered in the MDX output (expected behavior). 5. Each MDX file exports codeBlocks as a named constant for use by CopyButton/CopyAsPrompt/CodeTabs components.
